summary: The diff implements most requirements but has several notable gaps. The Motoko backend uses an authorization/access-control mixin that restricts getAppointments (getAllAppointments) to admins only and does not expose a public getAppointments function, which conflicts with REQ-10. Stable storage for canister upgrades is not implemented in the backend. The booking form requires Internet Identity authentication, effectively gating REQ-5 behind login. Framer Motion is not used anywhere — animations are implemented via CSS/IntersectionObserver instead. Several hallucinated features are present including Internet Identity integration, ProfileSetupModal, user profile save/get system, and an admin-only getAllAppointments function.

missing_requirements.requirement: Use Framer Motion for entrance animations in the hero section
missing_requirements.reason: The HeroSection uses CSS animation classes (hero-fade-in, hero-fade-in-delay-1, etc.) and a custom useInView hook, not Framer Motion. No Framer Motion import is present in HeroSection.tsx.

missing_requirements.requirement: Animate department cards on hover using Framer Motion
missing_requirements.reason: DepartmentsSection uses CSS hover classes (hover:-translate-y-2, hover:scale-[1.02]) and IntersectionObserver-based scroll animations, not Framer Motion.

missing_requirements.requirement: Appointment booking form should be accessible without authentication; form should not require login
missing_requirements.reason: BookingSection gates the form behind Internet Identity authentication. Unauthenticated users see a lock screen with a login prompt instead of the booking form. The spec states no user authentication system is required.

missing_requirements.requirement: Apply Framer Motion scroll-triggered entrance animations to feature cards
missing_requirements.reason: FeaturesSection uses a custom useInView hook with CSS animation classes, not Framer Motion.

missing_requirements.requirement: getAppointments function returns all stored appointments
missing_requirements.reason: The backend exposes getAllAppointments which is restricted to admin-only via AccessControl check. There is no public getAppointments function as specified.

missing_requirements.requirement: Appointments are persisted across canister upgrades using stable storage
missing_requirements.reason: The backend uses Map.empty and var nextId without stable var declarations, so data will not survive canister upgrades.

hallucinated_features.description: Internet Identity authentication integration with login/logout functionality wired to the booking form and profile system
hallucinated_features.reason: The build request explicitly lists user authentication as a non-goal and states Login/Register buttons are UI-only. The implementation fully wires up Internet Identity and gates the booking form behind login.

hallucinated_features.description: ProfileSetupModal component that prompts authenticated users to set up a name and email profile
hallucinated_features.reason: No profile setup modal was requested. This is an extra feature tied to the unauthorized authentication system.

hallucinated_features.description: User profile save and retrieve system in backend including UserProfile type, saveCallerUserProfile, getCallerUserProfile, and getUserProfile functions
hallucinated_features.reason: The spec only requires appointment booking functions in the backend. User profile management was not requested.

hallucinated_features.description: Admin-only access control with MixinAuthorization and AccessControl restricting appointment retrieval
hallucinated_features.reason: The spec requests simple public appointment functions. Admin access control was not requested and conflicts with the spec requirement for a public getAppointments function.
